data.title: Titanium Transportation agrees to go private in $2.22 per share deal
data.description:
• Titanium Transportation Group (TTNM:CA) on Thursday said that it has agreed to a going-private transaction under which minority shareholders will receive $2.22 per share in cash, valuing their holdings at a 41% premium to the stock’s last close.
  • The deal will be completed through a statutory plan of arrangement under Canadian law, with TTNM Management Acquisition Limited as purchaser and backing from major shareholder Trunkeast Investments Canada Limited. Management, Trunkeast and other “rolling shareholders,” who collectively own about 50.5% of the shares, will retain their equity and roll it into the private company.
  • Titanium’s special committee and board of directors, excluding conflicted members, unanimously approved the transaction and recommended shareholders vote in favor.
  • National Bank Capital Markets delivered a fairness opinion and a formal valuation that placed the fair market value of the shares between $2.20 and $2.70.
  • The transaction requires approval by two-thirds of votes cast, a majority of minority shareholders, and court approval.
  • Shareholders representing about 50.7% of the voting power have agreed to support the deal.
  • Titanium expects to hold a shareholder meeting in March 2026 and, if approved, to close shortly thereafter, followed by delisting from the Toronto Stock Exchange.
  • TTNMF closed -0.25% at $1.18.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Main Street Capital estimates Q4 earnings above consensus
data.description:
Main Street Capital (MAIN) on Thursday said its Q4 distributable net investment income is projected to be $1.07-$1.11 per share, vs. $0.99 consensus.

Shares were +0.37% pre-market to $62.50.

Net investment income is estimated to come in the range of $1.01 to $1.05 per share.

Preliminary estimate of net asset value per share as of December 31, 2025, is $33.29 to $33.37. The range represents an increase of $0.51 to $0.59 per share, or 1.5% to 1.8%, from the NAV per share of $32.78 as of September 30, 2025.

The increase in estimated NAV per share is primarily attributed to the net fair value increase of the investment portfolio and the accretive impact of equity issuances.

Main Street estimates that it generated a return on equity of over 17% for Q4 on an annualized basis and for the full year 2025.

The company is set to release its Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results on Thursday, February 26, 2026, after the financial markets close.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Goldman Sachs reports mixed Q4 results; trading revenue exceeds expectations
Manhattan Associates rises after Citi upgrades to Buy
data.description:
Citi upgraded Manhattan Associates' (MANH) rating to Buy from Neutral and raised the price target on the stock to $208 from $200.

Shares of Manhattan Associates — which develops software solutions to manage supply chains, inventory, and omni-channel operations — rose about 2% premarket on Thursday.

"We are upgrading MANH to Buy, seeing an opportunity for the company to capitalize on a renewal cycle in CY26-CY27 for the initial cohorts of cloud customers landed in 2020-2022. After accounting for an estimated $230M in RPO [Remaining Performance Obligation] additions from renewals for CY26, we see a low bar for bookings from new logos and conversion activity, with renewals coming at full ramp and with likely price step-ups, improving the line of sight to 20%+ cloud growth," said analysts led by George Kurosawa.

The analysts noted that their conversation with a platinum partner indicated a strong finish to the year and a healthy pipe into 2026. Following the 2025 reset, the analysts see an improved valuation profile and are opportunistically upgrading the rating to Buy and raising their price target.

The analysts said that after the CEO transition in February 2025, they have been positively surprised with the stability of the rest of the core executive team. The company added new product-level sales leaders in emerging product categories, with the hiring of Greg Betz as COO, with a mandate to build and scale teams dedicated to renewals and cloud conversions, which should be helpful to driving better cross-sell attach and a stronger renewal uplift, according to the analysts.

data.title: Community Financial System to buy ClearPoint in $40M deal
data.description:
Community Financial System’s (CBU) wholly owned banking subsidiary, Community Bank, N.A., has entered into an agreement to acquire ClearPoint Federal Bank & Trust.

ClearPoint is a federally chartered savings bank offering expertise to funeral homes and cemeteries nationwide, with over $1.5B of assets under management and a 3-year revenue CAGR of 8.8%.

The all-cash transaction is valued at $40M, subject to potential purchase price adjustment, and is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026.

The transaction significantly expands the revenue and offerings of Nottingham Financial Group ((NFG)), Community Financial’s wealth management services business, and contributes to its capital deployment into durable, recurring, growing income streams.

Following the consummation of the transaction, ClearPoint is expected to retain its brand and offerings, while benefiting from deeper capabilities as part of both NFG and Community Bank, N.A.

data.title: Goldman Sachs boost quarterly dividend by 12.5% to $4.50
data.description:
• Goldman Sachs (GS) declares $4.50/share quarterly dividend, 12.5% increase from prior dividend of $4.00.
  • Forward yield 1.93%
  • Payable March 30; for shareholders of record March 2; ex-div March 2.
  • See GS Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Clearway, Google sign 1.2 GW of power purchase agreements across three states
data.description:
Clearway Energy (CWEN) +7.1% pre-market Thursday after saying it signed three new power purchase agreements for up to 20 years with Google to deliver a combined 1.17 GW of energy to local grids to support Google data centers in Missouri, Texas, and West Virginia, representing more than $2.4B in new energy infrastructure.

Construction will begin this year on the projects totaling more than 1 GW, with the first sites expected to come online in 2027 and 2028.

The company said the new agreements build on Clearway (CWEN) and Google's existing 71.5 MW PPA in operation in West Virginia, bringing the total partnership to 1.24 GW.

"Our collaboration with Clearway will help power our data centers and the broader economic growth of communities within SPP, ERCOT, and PJM footprints," Google's Global Head of Data Center Energy Amanda Peterson Corio said.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Non-GAAP EPS of $14.01 beats by $2.25, revenue of $13.45B misses by $400M
data.description:
• Goldman Sachs press release (GS): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $14.01 beats by $2.25.
  • Revenue of $13.45B (-3.0% Y/Y) misses by $400M.
  • Net revenues were $13.45 billion for the fourth quarter of 2025, 3% lower than thefourth quarter of 2024 and 11% lower than the third quarter of 2025. The decreasecompared with the fourth quarter of 2024 reflected negative net revenues in PlatformSolutions (which reflected a reduction in net revenues of $2.26 billion frommarkdowns on the outstanding credit card portfolio related to the transfer of theApple Card loans to held for sale and contract termination obligations in connectionwith the agreement to transition the program to another issuer, which was more thanoffset by a related reserve reduction of $2.48 billion in provision for credit losses),largely offset by significantly higher net revenues in Global Banking & Markets.
  • Return on average common shareholders' equity was 15.0% for 2025 and annualized ROE was 16.0% for the fourthquarter of 2025.Book value per common share increased by 6.2% during 2025 and by 1.1% during the fourth quarter of 2025 to $357.60.

data.title: Morgan Stanley GAAP EPS of $2.68 beats by $0.23, revenue of $17.89B beats by $140M
data.description:
• Morgan Stanley press release (MS): Q4 GAAP EPS of $2.68 beats by $0.23.
  • Revenue of $17.89B (+10.4% Y/Y) beats by $140M.
  •  The Firm expense efficiency ratio was 68% compared to 71% a year ago, demonstratingoperating leverage while continuing to invest in our businesses.
